texto: There is, I believe, no person, however insignificant in the world, but, if an account of his life and adventures were committed to paper, would be entertaining in some degree: the follies of our own life, and those we are liable to be drawn into by others, will constantly afford matter for serious reflection.
explicacao: Citação de Henry Spencer Ashbee sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “There is, I believe, no person, however insignificant in the world, but, if an account of his lif…” articula vida, insignificance, reflection em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como vida e reflection.

frase_original: There is, I believe, no person, however insignificant in the world, but, if an account of his life and adventures were committed to paper, would be entertaining in some degree: the follies of our own life, and those we are liable to be drawn into by others, will constantly afford matter for serious reflection.
